description: Coordinated Gene Activity in Pattern Sets (CoGAPS) implements a Bayesian MCMC matrix factorization algorithm, GAPS, and links it to gene set statistic methods to infer biological process activity. It can be used to perform sparse matrix factorization on any data, and when this data represents biomolecules, to do gene set analysis. This package, PyCoGAPS, presents a unified Python interface, with a parallel, efficient underlying implementation.
author: Ashley Tsang, Johns-Hopkins - Fertig Lab, Ted Liefeld ;UCSD - Mesirov Lab
plan:
## PyCoGAPS GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
PyCoGAPS is a GenePattern module that Coordinated Gene Activity in Pattern Sets (CoGAPS) implements a Bayesian MCMC matrix factorization algorithm, GAPS, and links it to gene set statistic methods to infer biological process activity. It can be used to perform sparse matrix factorization on any data, and when this data represents biomolecules, to do gene set analysis. This package, PyCoGAPS, presents a unified Python interface, with a parallel, efficient underlying implementation.

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 39 parameters:
- Required: input.file, output.filename, num.patterns, num.iterations, seed, use.sparse.optimization, transpose.data, num.threads, messages, output.frequency, checkpoint.out.filename, worker.ID, asynchronous.updates, n.snapshots, snapshot.phase, alpha.A, alpha.P, max.Gibbs.Mass.A, max.Gibbs.Mass.P, num.sets, subset.dimension, take.pump.samples
- Optional: uncertainty, checkpoint.in.file, distributed, cut, min.NS, max.NS, explicit.sets, sampling.annotation, sampling.weight, subset.indices, gene.names, sample.names, fixed.patterns, which.matrix.fixed, hdf.key, hdf.row.key, hdf.col.key

### 3. Command Line
```
python3 /home/user/pycogaps-gpn/run_pycogaps.py  <input.file> <output.filename> <num.patterns> <num.iterations> <seed> <use.sparse.optimization> <num.threads> <messages> <output.frequency> <uncertainty> <checkpoint.out.filename> <checkpoint.in.file>  <worker.ID> <asynchronous.updates> <n.snapshots> <snapshot.phase> <alpha.A> <alpha.P> <max.Gibbs.Mass.A> <max.Gibbs.Mass.P> <distributed> <num.sets> <cut> <min.NS> <max.NS> <explicit.sets> <sampling.annotation> <sampling.weight> <subset.indices> <subset.dimension> <gene.names> <sample.names> <fixed.patterns> <which.matrix.fixed> <take.pump.samples> <hdf.key> <hdf.row.key> <hdf.col.key>  <transpose.data>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: fertiglab/pycogaps-gpn\:0.2.2

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: input.file
parameters.description: Input data file in h5ad, csv, gct or txt format.

parameters.name: output.filename
parameters.description: The result output file name (output is saved as a .h5ad file).\t

parameters.name: num.patterns
parameters.description: The number of patterns PyCoGAPS will learn.

parameters.name: num.iterations
parameters.description: The number of iterations for each phase of the algorithm.

parameters.name: seed
parameters.description: Random number generator seed.

parameters.name: use.sparse.optimization
parameters.description: When true, speeds up performance with sparse data (roughly >80% of data is zero), note this can only be used with the default uncertainty.

parameters.name: transpose.data
parameters.description: Transpose the dataset before processing.

parameters.name: num.threads
parameters.description: The maximum number of threads to run on.

parameters.name: messages
parameters.description: When True, display additional outputs to stdout.txt.

parameters.name: output.frequency
parameters.description: The number of iterations between each output (set to 0 to disable status updates).

parameters.name: uncertainty
parameters.description: The uncertainty matrix - in csv format.

parameters.name: checkpoint.out.filename
parameters.description: The name of the checkpoint file to create.

parameters.name: checkpoint.in.file
parameters.description: If this is provided, CoGAPS runs from the checkpoint contained in this file.

parameters.name: worker.ID
parameters.description: If calling CoGAPS in parallel the worker ID can be specified.

parameters.name: asynchronous.updates
parameters.description: Enable asynchronous updating which allows for multi-threaded runs.

parameters.name: n.snapshots
parameters.description: Sets how many snapshots to take in each phase, setting this to 0 disables snapshots.

parameters.name: snapshot.phase
parameters.description: During which phase to take snapshots in e.g. \u201cequilibration\u201d, \u201csampling\u201d, \u201call\u201d.

parameters.name: alpha.A
parameters.description: The sparsity parameter for feature matrix.

parameters.name: alpha.P
parameters.description: The sparsity parameter for sample matrix.

parameters.name: max.Gibbs.Mass.A
parameters.description: The atomic mass restriction for feature matrix.

parameters.name: max.Gibbs.Mass.P
parameters.description: The atomic mass restriction for sample matrix.

parameters.name: distributed
parameters.description: Either null (None) or genome-wide.

parameters.name: num.sets
parameters.description: The number of sets to break data into.

parameters.name: cut
parameters.description: The number of branches at which to cut dendrogram used in pattern matching.\n default\: nPatterns

parameters.name: min.NS
parameters.description: The minimum of individual set contributions a cluster must contain. default\: Math.ceil(cut / 2)

parameters.name: max.NS
parameters.description: The maximum of individual set contributions a cluster can contain.\n default\: minNS + nSets

parameters.name: explicit.sets
parameters.description: Whether to specify subsets by index or name.

parameters.name: sampling.annotation
parameters.description: Specify categories along the rows (cols) to use for weighted sampling.\t

parameters.name: sampling.weight
parameters.description: The weights associated with sampling annotation.\t

parameters.name: subset.indices
parameters.description: The set of indices to use from the data.\t

parameters.name: subset.dimension
parameters.description: Which dimension (rows, columns) to subset.\t

parameters.name: gene.names
parameters.description: The vector of names of genes in the data.\t

parameters.name: sample.names
parameters.description: The vector of names of samples in the data.\t

parameters.name: fixed.patterns
parameters.description: The provided fixedPatterns matrix (either \u2018A\u2019 or \u2018P\u2019 matrix) allows for manual pattern matching. In distributed CoGAPS (distributed\=\u2019genome-wide\u2019), the first phase is skipped and fixedPatterns is used for all sets. In standard CoGAPS, fixedPatterns allows for fixed runs.

parameters.name: which.matrix.fixed
parameters.description: Either \u2018A\u2019 or \u2018P\u2019, indicating which matrix is fixed.\t

parameters.name: take.pump.samples
parameters.description: Whether or not to take PUMP samples.\t

parameters.name: hdf.key
parameters.description: Hdf key for reading .h5 files.\t

parameters.name: hdf.row.key
parameters.description: Hdf row key for reading .h5 files.\t

parameters.name: hdf.col.key
parameters.description: Hdf column key for reading .h5 files.\t
